title: Cryptogramma heterophylla
taxonomy: Cryptogramma heterophylla is classified as a species of fern in the genus Cryptogramma. It belongs to the family Pteridaceae. The genus Cryptogramma is part of the order Polypodiales. This plant is native to North America, specifically in the western regions. It is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Cryptogramma heterophylla, also known as Mountain Rockcress or Wood's Cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, Mountain Rockcress has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include isothiocyanates, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Mountain Rockcress is often prepared in herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and treat respiratory infections.
